blocker_detail: AMD Instinct MI300X OAM is the only AMD GPU SKU listed in RunPod's datacenter inventory (EU-RO-1, Romania); 8 pod-create attempts spanning ~8 minutes all returned `no longer any instances available with the requested specifications`. Probed alternate SKUs (MI250X / MI250 / MI210 / MI100 / Radeon Pro V520) — none exist in the RunPod catalog. SECURE + COMMUNITY cloud-types both empty. No orphan pods left behind (failed creates do not provision).
cost_breakdown: Pod never created; zero spend. RunPod MI300X list price is $2.49/hr (cited from public RunPod pricing page; not metered here).
honest_scope: RFC 075 ROCm P4 closure blocked by external GPU-pool inventory. Hand-emit HIP kernel + host harness (vec_add.cpp + host_setup.sh) authored and ready to fire the instant MI300X stock returns or an alternate ROCm GPU pool is found. Code parity with Metal P4 fire (commit 4415ec91): same MIR pattern, same LCG-deterministic FP32 inputs, same byte-eq assertion. Falsifier F-RFC075-ROCM-NUMERIC-EQ remains UNRUN — explicitly NOT marked PASS. Single-session honest punt; multi-session follow-up: (a) retry RunPod MI300X on stock-return; (b) probe vast.ai / Lambda Labs / Hot Aisle for MI250/MI300 inventory; (c) user-local AMD workstation if available.
next_steps: Re-run this cycle when `runpodctl datacenter list` shows MI300X stockStatus != '' (e.g. 'Low' / 'Medium' / 'High')
Or probe vast.ai for AMD Instinct rentals (separate CLI)
Or wait for user-local Linux+AMD GPU pool host (currently ubu-1/ubu-2 are NVIDIA-only per pool config)
